H008.title: BOS + FVG confluence entry (EURUSD/XAUUSD specific)
H008.analysis: EURUSD+XAUUSD show consistent +5.4pp edge on 259 combined setups (p=0.23). GBPUSD shows no edge (-1.6pp). Pattern appears symbol-specific. Statistical significance requires n≥600 setups which needs ~8yr H1 data OR higher frequency (M5) data. Edge is too small at +5.4pp to reach significance with 2yr data.
H008.lesson: BOS+FVG is NOT a universal edge — GBPUSD behaves differently. But EURUSD+XAUUSD show consistent direction. Before abandoning: try H008b with tighter filters to raise WR to 60%+
H008.next_step: H008b: Add session filter (London open BOS only) + ATR quality filter to target WR>60% on EURUSD+XAUUSD subset
H008.conclusion: CLOSED by H008c. The 55.2% NEEDS_MORE_DATA signal was an artifact of shallow, H1-resampled (not real M15) data. On real deep M15 (EURUSD ~1yr, XAUUSD ~3yr), with look-ahead removed (causal swing confirmation) and a chronological out-of-sample split, pooled TEST WR=0.489 (n=562, p=0.83) — at the H001 coin-flip baseline. The London+ATR filter made it WORSE OOS (0.422). BOS+FVG has no standalone directional edge. SMC/ICT concepts survive only as confluence filters, never as primary entries (consistent with H001/H002/H002b).

H008b.title: BOS+FVG + London session (02-10 UTC) + ATR×1.5 filter
H008b.reason: Filters too restrictive. Session filter removes 64-78% of setups, ATR filter removes 19-35% more. Final n=0-9 across all symbols (need n≥50). H1 hourly data doesn't have enough London-session BOS+FVG patterns in 2yr period. Would need M15 data or wider session window to get adequate sample.
H008b.lesson: BOS+FVG works as CONFLUENCE FILTER (already in SMC engine implicitly) but not as standalone primary signal on H1 data. The pattern is too rare to test statistically with available data.

H012.title: COT + Retail Sentiment Engine
H012.notes: Paper trading. REAL COT now wired: scripts/download_cot.py fetches CFTC legacy futures-only weekly (free) into data/cot/{SYMBOL}.json which the engine already consumes; engine._symbol wired in main.build_active_engines (was 'UNKNOWN', so COT could never load). Engine stays DISABLED — real data is a prerequisite for evaluating H012, not a license to enable it.
H012.data_collection: GAP IDENTIFIED 2026-07-24 (research/results/data_feasibility_report.md Part B.2): scripts/download_cot.py's 12-week rolling cache cannot support a chronological OOS split. CLOSED same day: scripts/download_cot_deep_history.py, same free official CFTC source (yearly deacotYYYY.zip archives, https://www.cftc.gov/files/dea/history/, URL pattern verified against the working open-source cot_reports library, not guessed), reuses download_cot.py's row-matching logic (refactored into a shared iter_cot_rows() generator). Writes to data/cot/{SYMBOL}_deep_history.json, deliberately separate from the live rolling-cache file so the two collectors never fight. Ships a --probe mode (fetches one year, prints diagnostics, writes nothing).
H012.data_quality_bug_found_and_fixed: 2026-07-24, VPS --probe run against the real 2025 CFTC archive: EURUSD returned 120 weekly rows instead of the expected ~52 — iter_cot_rows()'s bare market.startswith(contract.upper()) also matched CFTC's separately-listed EUR cross-rate contracts ('EURO FX/BRITISH POUND XRATE - ...', 'EURO FX/JAPANESE YEN XRATE - ...', 52+16 extra rows) and, for XAUUSD, an unrelated Coinbase Derivatives 1oz gold contract ('GOLD -1 TROY OUNCE - COINBASE DERIVATIVES, LLC'). This bug PREDATES the deep-history script and was already live in scripts/download_cot.py's weekly current-file collector via the same shared matching function — data/cot/EURUSD.json's production cache (running since 2026-07-09) may have silently held whichever contaminating contract's row landed last in a given week's file order, not necessarily the real EURO FX net position, though this was never consumed by any live trading decision since the Sentiment engine has stayed DISABLED throughout. FIXED same day: matching now requires the exact CFTC field delimiter (contract.upper() + ' - ', i.e. the real 'CONTRACT NAME - EXCHANGE NAME' format) instead of a bare prefix — verified to exclude both contaminating contracts while keeping the real ones (3 new regression tests, tests/test_cot_download.py, using the exact real market-name strings observed on the VPS). RE-PROBED 2026-07-24 after deploying the delimiter fix: EURUSD/XAUUSD both correctly returned exactly 52 rows — confirms the fix. That re-probe surfaced two FURTHER findings, both from real data, both now fixed in engines/sentiment_engine.py's COT_SYMBOLS: (1) NZDUSD's mapped name 'NEW ZEALAND DOLLAR' matched zero rows all year — a broader substring search on the VPS found the real 2025 contract is listed as 'NZ DOLLAR - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE' (a CFTC rename, not a matching-logic bug); COT_SYMBOLS updated to 'NZ DOLLAR'. (2) USOIL's mapped name 'CRUDE OIL, LIGHT SWEET' was, even before the delimiter fix, ONLY ever bare-prefix-matching a DIFFERENT, unintended contract at a different venue — 'CRUDE OIL, LIGHT SWEET-WTI - ICE FUTURES EUROPE' — while the real NYMEX/US-benchmark WTI contract is listed as 'WTI FINANCIAL CRUDE OIL - NEW YORK MERCANTILE EXCHANGE'. This means USOIL's COT data, for its entire existence since the 2026-07-09 wiring, was silently tracking European ICE positioning, not the intended NYMEX contract — a genuine, previously-invisible wrong-source bug, not merely a contamination/over-matching one. JUDGMENT CALL, flagged rather than silently applied: COT_SYMBOLS['USOIL'] changed to 'WTI FINANCIAL CRUDE OIL' (the NYMEX/domestic contract) on the reasoning that 'USOIL' as used throughout this project denotes the US WTI benchmark, not the European ICE contract — this should be treated as a reviewable decision, not a certainty, since no prior documentation in this repo explicitly specified which venue was intended. Also found and fixed in the same pass: scripts/download_cot.py's live current-week fetch had no User-Agent header and was returning HTTP 403 from cftc.gov (confirmed via a direct real-network test: identical request with a User-Agent succeeded, 410KB); scripts/download_cot_deep_history.py already set one and never hit this — added the same header to the live collector. 3 new regression tests (tests/test_cot_download.py) pin both contract-name corrections. Production cache (data/cot/*.json) was cleared and should be rebuilt fresh post-fix; the Sentiment engine has been DISABLED throughout, so none of these bugs affected any live trading decision.
H012.backfill_verification: Full 1986-2025 backfill run on the VPS (2026-07-24) after the fixes above, then sanity-checked per symbol — this surfaced ONE MORE real finding the 2025-only probe could not have caught: GBPUSD and NZDUSD both returned suspiciously short depth (204 weekly records each, both starting on the exact identical date 2022-02-08), while EUR/JPY/CHF/CAD/GOLD/SILVER all correctly got the full ~1896-1898 records back to 1986. The identical count AND identical start date across two unrelated currencies was too specific to be coincidence. Investigated via a targeted 2015-archive probe: confirmed CFTC used LONGER contract names before some renaming event between 2015 and 2022 — 'BRITISH POUND STERLING - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE' (not 'BRITISH POUND') and 'NEW ZEALAND DOLLAR - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE' (the exact name already tried and discarded earlier in this same investigation, on the mistaken assumption it was simply stale rather than one of two valid eras). FIXED: COT_SYMBOLS restructured from {internal: single_name} to {internal: tuple_of_aliases} — engines/sentiment_engine.py, scripts/download_cot.py::iter_cot_rows() now checks every alias per symbol. GBPUSD -> ('BRITISH POUND', 'BRITISH POUND STERLING'), NZDUSD -> ('NZ DOLLAR', 'NEW ZEALAND DOLLAR'). Final post-fix backfill depths (VPS, 2026-07-24): USDCAD/USDJPY/XAUUSD/XAGUSD/USDCHF ~1896-1898 records (1986-2025, full house-standard depth); EURUSD 1409 records (1999-2025, correctly bounded by the Euro's actual 1999 launch, not a bug); BTCUSD 404 records (2018-2025, correctly bounded by CME Bitcoin futures' 2018 launch); AUDUSD 1147 records (2004-2025) - UNCONFIRMED whether this is a genuine reporting-threshold start date or yet another undiscovered pre-2004 alias, flagged as an open item, lower priority since it lacks the GBP/NZD 'identical count+date' forensic signal.
H012.backfill_verification_rerun: Re-run 2026-07-24 on the VPS with the alias fix deployed. CONFIRMED FIXED: GBPUSD now spans 1986-01-15 to 2025-12-30 (1206 records) — the pre-2022 alias closed the truncation. NZDUSD now spans 2004-08-17 to 2025-12-30 (1062 records) — extended far past its prior 2022-truncated state, but did NOT reach back to 1986 like GBP did. TWO OPEN ITEMS recorded, neither blocking (H012's stated minimum is ~100-150 observations; every symbol clears this by a wide margin): (1) GBPUSD's record COUNT (1206) is only ~64% of the ~1897 the 'clean' full-depth symbols (USDCHF/XAGUSD/USDJPY/USDCAD/XAUUSD) show over the IDENTICAL 1986-2025 span — the truncation bug is fixed (correct date range now), but there may be real gaps within that range not yet investigated. (2) NZDUSD's 2004-08-17 start is close to but not identical to AUDUSD's 2004-01-06 (unlike the GBP/NZD 2022 case's identical-date fingerprint) — weaker evidence of a further undiscovered alias, more consistent with a genuine reporting-start date for both Oceania currencies; folded into the same lower-priority open-item bucket as AUDUSD rather than investigated further. Both items are DOCUMENTED, NOT FIXED — H012's data acquisition is considered materially closed at this depth (the three confirmed, high-impact bugs — cross-contract contamination, wrong-venue USOIL, GBP/NZD 2022 truncation — are fixed and verified); revisit the two open items only if a future H012 A/B run's results look sensitive to them.

H013.title: Reversal Engine Group Agreement as Counter-Signal
H013.status_caveat: Registry hygiene fix (2026-07-14): this has been LIVE in production since 2026-06-27 (confluence/reversal_veto.py, main.py, use_reversal_veto=True in BacktestConfig) but was still labeled RESEARCH here — corrected to reflect reality. Marked PASSED rather than a stronger label deliberately: the only evidence is ONE observed case (2026-06-26), not an OOS-tested rule. Per research/edge_gate.py's audit_passed_hypotheses, a PASSED entry with no evidence block (oos_trades/oos_pf/walk_forward/monte_carlo) gets flagged at every boot as failing PROMOTION_CRITERIA — intentional here, since n=1 genuinely does not justify a live veto and this should stay flagged until properly measured.
H013.description: When 3+ reversal engines (Divergence, Wyckoff, Sentiment) all agree on opposite direction to trend engines, this may indicate imminent reversal. First observed 2026-06-26: 4 BEARISH trend engines vs 3 BULLISH reversal engines — market reversed BULLISH confirming reversal engines.
H013.hypothesis: If Divergence+Wyckoff+Sentiment all agree on direction X, and trend engines agree on -X, lower confidence or block the trade.

rules.hard_veto: 3/3 reversal engines unanimously oppose → BLOCK trade
rules.soft_veto: 2/3 reversal engines oppose → reduce score by 50%

H014.title: Engine Orthogonality Test
H014.description: Measure pairwise correlation between engine vote directions. If SMC↔ICT correlation > 0.85, one is redundant. The goal is not more engines but more INDEPENDENT engines.
H014.conclusion: Informed by the independence matrix (ablation_20260703.json) + H015: engines are non-redundant (pairwise agreement mostly 27-76%); no engine pair is collinear enough to drop for orthogonality reasons. No change warranted.

H015.title: Ablation Study — Minimum Engine Set
H015.description: What is the smallest set of engines that maintains PF >= 1.15? If 5 engines give PF=1.17 and 9 engines give PF=1.15, the extra 4 engines add noise not signal.
H015.conclusion: CLOSED (2026-07-10, 15-symbol confirmation). The pre-registered rule held: selected-7 beat prod4 on TEST mean-PF (1.0398 vs 0.8695, delta +0.170) but won only 9/15 symbols (< the required 2/3) -> KEEP prod4. Decisive observation: the 'better subset' is UNSTABLE across universes — the 3-symbol search chose {ict,nnfx,pa,quant,wyckoff}, the 15-symbol search chose {divergence,nnfx,pa,quant,sentiment,smc,wyckoff} (including the sentiment price-proxy, a noise-fitting tell). Universe-dependent selection = no robust engine-set improvement exists; prod4 is kept by burden-of-proof, not by being measured optimal. Stable signals across ALL runs: nnfx and price_action are load-bearing (dropping either always hurts); drop-smc helps mildly in-train everywhere but no alternative containing it survives the adoption rule OOS. SECONDARY FINDING (flagged to the operator): prod4 TEST-window (~mid-2025 to mid-2026) mean-PF across 15 symbols = 0.87 — the FX book drags below breakeven while carriers (XAU 1.33, BTC 1.37) stay positive, matching the philosophy audit's core verdict. Results: engine_subset_search.json (3-sym) + engine_subset_search_15sym.json + 20260710 manifest (clean tree).

H016.title: Engine Pair Synergy Analysis
H016.description: Evaluate WR when engine pairs AGREE vs DISAGREE. If Wyckoff+Divergence agreement gives 45% WR vs 28% when they disagree, this pair has high synergy lift.
H016.conclusion: Subsumed by H015's subset search: no engine pair addition to prod4 survives OOS. Pair-synergy pruning offers no OOS PF gain over the current 4.

H008c.title: BOS+FVG re-test — look-ahead-free + out-of-sample on real deep M15
H008c.tested_on: EURUSD ~35k M15 (1yr) + XAUUSD ~80k M15 (3yr), pooled test n=562
H008c.lesson: Removing look-ahead (causal swings) and splitting OOS collapses the apparent BOS+FVG edge to the coin-flip baseline. The session/ATR filter's 60% WR was in-sample overfitting (train n=11 63.6% -> test 35.5%).
H008c.conclusion: ABANDON the SMC-entry direction. Definitively no standalone edge.

H017.title: SMC full-spec internal confluence (order blocks + FVG + BOS/CHoCH as score modulators)
H017.notes: Implemented causally behind engines.smc_full_spec (default FALSE — live voting unchanged). Components modulate SMC's structural score only; never standalone entries (H001/H002/H008c measured no standalone edge: pooled OOS WR 0.489, p=0.83). Liquidity sweeps deliberately NOT implemented (failed 3 hypotheses). Decision rule pre-registered in scripts/smc_fullspec_ab.py: adopt only if chronological-TEST mean PF improves >= 0.03 with <= 1 losing symbol.
H017.conclusion: FAILED by the pre-registered rule (2026-07-10): chronological-TEST mean dPF = -0.04 (needed >= +0.03) with 3/4 losing symbols (allowed <= 1). TRAIN showed the usual mirage (XAUUSD +0.062 flipped to -0.190 OOS). ETHUSD's +0.536 (n=55) is a single-symbol outlier — enabling per-symbol would be the exact selection bias the audit condemns. engines.smc_full_spec stays FALSE; the causal detectors remain in the codebase for any future re-test on deeper data. Consistent with H008c: SMC concepts add nothing even as internal confluence, not just as entries.

H018.title: Structure-based stop placement (SL at OB/swing level instead of raw ATRx2.5)
H018.notes: PRE-REGISTERED ONLY — no code runs. The exit side has never received the hypothesis treatment the entries got (17 hypotheses on entries, zero on exits; current exits are ATRx2.5 / fixed RR). Design: reuse the H017 causal detectors (order-block bottom / confirmed swing low) as the SL anchor; TP keeps the RR floor. Decision rule (written before any result): controlled A/B like H017 (scripts/smc_fullspec_ab.py pattern), chronological TEST slice, adopt only if mean dPF >= +0.05 with <= 1 losing symbol of 4+. DO NOT START until the forward demo sample reaches ~100 closed trades — changing exits mid-sample would corrupt the only prospective evidence being accumulated (D002).

H019.title: Crypto positioning/sentiment as an internal confluence modulator (funding rate + open interest + Fear & Greed Index) — BTCUSD/ETHUSD only
H019.scope: Crypto only. Funding rate and open interest are perpetual-futures-specific and Fear & Greed is a crypto-market-wide index — neither concept has an FX/metals analogue, so this hypothesis cannot and does not extend beyond BTCUSD/ETHUSD, unlike H017/H018.
H019.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT a re-run of the crypto_volume experiment (dead list: ccxt raw traded volume fed into existing engines, DeltaPF -0.016 BTC / 0.000 ETH). That test measured trade volume; H019 tests derived positioning/sentiment signals never previously measured — funding-rate sign+magnitude as a crowding/squeeze proxy, OI trend as a conviction proxy, Fear&Greed extremes as a contrarian regime input. If this distinction stops holding once the detector is designed, the hypothesis should be withdrawn rather than run.
H019.notes: PRE-REGISTERED ONLY — no code runs. Architecture follows the H017 precedent exactly: a causal score modulator behind a new default-FALSE config flag (engines.crypto_positioning_modulator), applied only to engines already voting on BTCUSD/ETHUSD; never a standalone entry signal (every standalone-entry variant of every prior idea in this registry — H001/H002/H002b/H008/H008b/H008c — failed). Does not touch entries/exits/thresholds on any currently live symbol or engine, so it is NOT subject to the H018-style forward-sample freeze — it may be built and backtest-A/B'd at any time without corrupting the D001/D002 forward evidence counter. Deliberately deferred (registration only, implementation not started) per the 2026-07-11 audit follow-up: this cycle's priority is forward-evidence accumulation and operational data-provider verification, not new modulators.

data_sources.funding_rate: Binance perpetual futures via ccxt fetchFundingRateHistory — free, multi-year depth, no auth required. LOOK-AHEAD GUARD (required, not optional): the backtest may only use the funding rate PUBLISHED strictly before the decision bar's close (Binance settles on a fixed 8h schedule — 00:00/08:00/16:00 UTC); never a rate whose settlement timestamp is at or after the decision bar. If funding/OI are sourced as pre-aggregated daily files rather than fetched per-timestamp, per-bar alignment must be re-derived causally from raw timestamps — no forward-fill from a future aggregate. This is the same class of bug the trade-management '+100%' mirage and the raw H008 BOS+FVG result both turned out to be.
data_sources.open_interest: ccxt fetchOpenInterestHistory (Binance/Bybit) — free tier historical depth is UNVERIFIED and likely shallow (public OI endpoints commonly cap at recent weeks). MUST be validated as a standalone data-availability check before the full study starts; if depth is insufficient for a chronological OOS split, drop OI from this hypothesis rather than substitute a shorter/biased window. PARTIAL CHECK RUN 2026-07-11 (audit sandbox, not the VPS): api.binance.com and api.bybit.com both unreachable from this sandbox's network egress (HTTP 451 / 403 respectively — consistent with an exchange-side datacenter-IP geo-block, not a data-depth problem; the production VPS already operationally fetches Binance H4/D1 via ccxt per the existing crypto provider chain, so this is a sandbox artifact, not evidence against feasibility). The actual OI-depth question remains OPEN and must be re-run from the VPS before any H019 code is written.
data_sources.fear_greed_index: alternative.me public API — free, daily history since 2018-02, no auth required. REACHABILITY CONFIRMED 2026-07-11 (audit sandbox): HTTP 200, valid daily value+classification payload returned. ROLE, defined now rather than left implicit: Fear & Greed enters this study strictly as a REGIME FILTER / market-context input (e.g. gating or scaling the modulator's effect in extreme-fear / extreme-greed regimes) — it is explicitly NOT a symbol-specific directional signal for BTC vs ETH individually, since it is one shared time series feeding two symbols. Any design that treats it as per-symbol conviction must justify why first.

H019.decision_rule: Controlled A/B, same bars/config, chronological TEST split (H017/H018 method), symbols BTCUSD + ETHUSD (the only two eligible — n=2, not the 4+ used elsewhere). Because n=2 cannot support a '<=1 losing symbol' tolerance, the bar is stricter by construction: adopt ONLY if chronological-TEST mean PF improves >= 0.05 across BTCUSD+ETHUSD AND NEITHER symbol regresses (0 losing symbols of 2). A single losing symbol is FAILED outright — no per-symbol enabling (the exact selection bias H017's ETHUSD n=55 outlier already illustrated in this registry). Written before any result exists.
H019.implementation: Built 2026-07-24: confluence/crypto_positioning_modulator.py (pure, causal squeeze-risk penalty — funding-rate z-score vs trailing history, capped at -10, Fear&Greed as a same-direction amplifier only, never a standalone directional signal, never positive) wired into main.py behind engines.crypto_positioning_modulator (config/engines.yaml, default FALSE) via main._crypto_positioning_adjustment, which only ever reads an INJECTED context — no live funding-rate/Fear-Greed feed exists, so the flag is provably inert in live trading today regardless of its value (tests/test_crypto_positioning_wiring.py pins this directly). Data download: scripts/download_crypto_positioning_history.py. A/B harness: research/experiments/H019_crypto_positioning_ab.py, using the main.run_pipeline()-based pattern (H023/H037 style) per operator decision 2026-07-24 — explicitly NOT backtesting/backtest_engine.py (the literal 'H017 precedent' this hypothesis's architecture notes point to), because that engine is missing the live meta_decision gate entirely and would test a system that doesn't match production. Not yet run — needs scripts/download_all_symbols.py's BTCUSD/ETHUSD price CSVs plus scripts/download_crypto_positioning_history.py's funding-rate CSVs, both VPS-only.
H019.feasibility_probe: scripts/probe_crypto_positioning_data.py, three exchanges tested on the VPS 2026-07-24 (Binance, Bybit, OKX — all via ccxt, free public endpoints, no new provider integration). CLOSED — FUNDING RATE: Binance is the confirmed source. Properly paginated (7 requests/symbol): BTCUSD and ETHUSD both return 6574 records spanning 2020-07-24 to today (~2191 days, ~6 years), no gaps — deep enough for any chronological OOS split this registry's house standard requires. (Bybit: 1400 records/~5.3y but sparser; OKX: only 282 records/~94 days — Binance wins outright.) CLOSED — FEAR & GREED: excellent, 3092 daily records, full history 2018-02-01 to today, no gaps. CLOSED — OPEN INTEREST: DROPPED from H019, operator decision 2026-07-24, applying the hypothesis's own pre-registered fallback ('if depth is insufficient... drop OI rather than substitute a shorter/biased window'). Final measured depth, all three exchanges, correctly paginated: Binance ~7 days (hard exchange-side limit, confirmed twice, error -1130 past that window), Bybit ~199 days, OKX ~179 days — none reach the multi-year depth an institutional-grade walk-forward study needs, and the two borderline candidates (Bybit/OKX, both ~6 months) are marginal AND sourced from exchanges other than the project's primary crypto data provider (Binance via ccxt, config.yaml's provider_chains), adding a cross-exchange consistency question on top of the shallow-window problem. Operator explicitly considered and rejected introducing a paid provider (CryptoQuant/CoinGlass/Glassnode/Kaiko/Amberdata/Tardis.dev all researched — every free tier among them is similarly shallow or requires a paid plan) as disproportionate to what OI would add, consistent with this project's free-data-only discipline (CLAUDE.md data layer notes). H019 proceeds with funding rate (Binance) + Fear & Greed (alternative.me) only — feasibility fully resolved, both legs confirmed deep and clean. Ready for the A/B harness per the hypothesis's own architecture notes (H017 precedent: causal score modulator behind a new default-FALSE config flag).
H019.conclusion: FAILED by the pre-registered rule (2026-07-24, VPS, chronological TEST slice): BTCUSD arm A PF=1.246 n=94 -> arm B PF=1.115 n=96, dPF=-0.131 (regressed). ETHUSD arm A PF=1.141 n=92 -> arm B PF=1.199 n=91, dPF=+0.058 (improved). Mean dPF=-0.037, well below the +0.05 bar, and BTCUSD alone regressing is disqualifying under H019's own stricter-than-usual rule (n=2 cannot tolerate any losing symbol, unlike the '<=1 losing symbol' pattern used on larger symbol sets elsewhere in this registry). Not the 'arm A == arm B' data artifact seen on H023's first run — dPF is clearly nonzero and DIFFERENT in sign between the two symbols, meaning the modulator had a real, differentiated effect; it just was not a net-beneficial or even a consistent one. Plausible reading: BTC and ETH's funding-rate/crowding dynamics don't transfer uniformly to a single squeeze-risk formula — exactly the kind of engine-specific idiosyncrasy this registry's dead list has repeatedly found for cross-asset generalizations. engines.crypto_positioning_modulator stays FALSE (already the shipped default — no config change needed). The modulator code, causal alignment, and A/B harness remain in the codebase for any future re-test with a different formula (a NEW hypothesis, not a rerun of H019).

H020.title: Is confluence.min_informative_weight_share (Axis-8 'SPEAKING panel' gate, currently 0.6) net-negative for the FX book specifically, while neutral-to-positive for carriers?
H020.notes: REFUTED by the pre-registered controlled A/B (2026-07-13). None of the three required conditions held: TEST-slice FX mean dPF=+0.005 (need >=0.03), FX losing pairs=3/7 (need <=1), carrier mean |dPF|=0.056 (need <=0.02) — the gate actually swings the CARRIERS more than the FX book (BTCUSD alone: -0.095), the opposite of the hypothesis. Per-symbol dPF is a mixed bag with no asset-class pattern (FX: -0.042 to +0.049; carriers: -0.095 to +0.063). CORRECTION to H009's addendum_2026-07-13 and docs/STRATEGY_EVIDENCE_2026-07.md: the config-fingerprint diff that motivated this hypothesis (min_informative_weight_share as the sole behavior_blocks difference between the 2026-07-05 and 2026-07-13 manifests) does NOT explain the FX PF discrepancy under a controlled test — that discrepancy's actual cause remains OPEN/unexplained. VERDICT: NO ACTION — no config change, no follow-up asset-class-scoping work.
H020.method: Controlled A/B, same bars/config, chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%) split — H017's method (scripts/smc_fullspec_ab.py pattern) with confluence.min_informative_weight_share as the single flipped variable: 0.0 (pre-2026-07-05 baseline, effectively disabled) vs 0.6 (current). Symbols: the 7 currently-enabled FX pairs (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY, USDCHF, EURJPY, GBPJPY, AUDJPY) plus the 3 carriers (XAUUSD, BTCUSD, ETHUSD) as the control group — the gate must not be judged on FX alone, since a change would currently apply globally (there is no per-asset-class scoping of this parameter in the codebase today).
H020.decision_rule: Written before any result exists. Let dPF = PF(gate=0.0) minus PF(gate=0.6), TEST slice only, per symbol. VERDICT 'FX-NEGATIVE — WORTH ASSET-CLASS SCOPING' only if ALL THREE hold: (1) mean dPF across the 7 FX pairs >= +0.03, (2) at most 1 of 7 FX pairs shows dPF < 0, (3) mean |dPF| across the 3 carriers <= 0.02 (gate is roughly neutral for carriers, so removing it for FX would not sacrifice carrier performance if the parameter were ever made asset-class-specific). Otherwise VERDICT 'NO ACTION' — either the gate is not clearly FX-negative, or it also matters for carriers, and a scoped change would not obviously be safe. This hypothesis authorizes ONLY a verdict + optional follow-up hypothesis for an asset-class-scoped config change — it does NOT itself change config.yaml, and no config change may be made off the back of this result alone without its own pre-registration and forward-demo-safe rollout (CLAUDE.md: never change entries/exits/thresholds mid-sample).

H021.title: Does MarketAux news sentiment, fed into the (currently disabled) Sentiment engine, improve confluence performance enough to justify re-enabling it?
H021.method: Controlled A/B, H017/H020 methodology — same bars/config, chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%) split, ONE variable flipped: Sentiment engine disabled (current frozen baseline: smc, price_action, nnfx, wyckoff) vs Sentiment engine enabled with fundamentals/marketaux_client.py's news-sentiment score folded into engines/sentiment_engine.py as an additional signal alongside its existing COT + retail-proxy logic. confluence.weights.sentiment (0.0303) is already reserved in config.yaml even while disabled, so no re-normalization is needed to test this. Primary group: the 3 frozen carrier symbols (XAUUSD, BTCUSD, ETHUSD) — where the project's only measured edge lives, so they get first claim on the decision. Secondary/safety group: the 7 enabled FX pairs, reported for transparency but not a condition for adoption (the FX book is independently at/below breakeven regardless of this engine). ACKNOWLEDGED METHOD CONSTRAINT (declared before any data collection, so it cannot be used to justify a favorable result after the fact): MarketAux's free tier serves recent news articles, not a reconstructed historical sentiment time series spanning years like the H4 deep-history bars used elsewhere — and its rate limit (100 req/day) means building even a modest historical window requires a dedicated slow-collection script running over many days before a backtest is possible at all. The TRAIN/TEST window for this hypothesis will therefore be much shorter than H020's, and n will likely be low — if the collected sample is too small for the decision rule to apply meaningfully, the correct verdict is 'INSUFFICIENT DATA', not a forced ADOPT/REJECT call.
H021.decision_rule: Written before any result exists. Let dPF = PF(sentiment=ON) minus PF(sentiment=OFF), TEST slice only, per symbol. ADOPT (flip config/engines.yaml sentiment: true) only if ALL THREE hold on the carrier group: (1) carrier mean dPF >= +0.05, (2) no individual carrier's PF drops by more than 0.03, (3) total EXECUTE-signal count across the 3 carriers does not drop by more than 20% vs baseline (an overly conservative sentiment veto that merely starves trade frequency is not a genuine improvement, even if the surviving trades look better in isolation). If the collected sentiment sample is too small (fewer than ~30 sentiment-informed decisions per carrier symbol on the TEST slice) to evaluate these conditions meaningfully, VERDICT is 'INSUFFICIENT DATA' — no config change, revisit once more data is collected. Otherwise: any of conditions 1-3 failing -> REJECT, sentiment engine stays disabled, negative result documented with the same care as a positive one (CLAUDE.md rule 4). An ADOPT verdict from this backtest alone does not constitute full validation — per CLAUDE.md, a config change affecting live trading still needs forward-demo-safe rollout, same as H018 staying FROZEN pending forward evidence.
H021.notes: Pre-registered 2026-07-14 at operator request, before fundamentals/marketaux_client.py's output was wired into any scoring logic. Distinct from H012 (COT + retail-proxy sentiment, same engine, different data source, already RESEARCH status) — this hypothesis is scoped ONLY to the incremental question of adding MarketAux news sentiment on top. Client + engine wiring may exist in the codebase (config-gated, off by default) before this hypothesis resolves; existence of the code is not evidence and does not change engines.yaml's sentiment: false default.
H021.data_collection: scripts/collect_marketaux_sentiment.py + iatis-marketaux-collect.timer (2026-07-24) — the accumulation step this hypothesis's own method notes call for (free tier serves recent news only, no historical backfill; a backtestable sample only exists after running this repeatedly over time). Appends to data/marketaux_sentiment_log.jsonl, gitignored like other historical datasets. XAUUSD mapping RESOLVED 2026-07-24: --probe-xauusd against the live API found 'GOLD' returns real entity matches (3/3 articles matched); 'XAUUSD', 'XAU/USD', 'XAU' all returned zero. fundamentals/marketaux_client.py's MARKETAUX_SYMBOL_MAP now maps XAUUSD -> GOLD — all three priority carrier symbols (XAUUSD, BTCUSD, ETHUSD) are covered. Timer not yet enabled on the VPS as of this note — enable it to start the clock on data accumulation.

H022.title: FX-cross universe expansion: do USDCNH, GBPAUD and EURAUD carry the same H4 confluence edge, at real broker cost, on deep out-of-sample history?
H022.origin: The 2026-07-17 full-universe IC Markets re-cost sweep (ic_symbols_backtest_20260717 manifest; get_spot_by_name gave EVERY candidate a real measured spread for the first time). Of 60 nominal winners, the alt-coin block was DISQUALIFIED on methodology, not opinion: re-fetching a fresh 1200-bar window one day apart swung their PF by more than +/-1.0 at n~25-35 (ENAUSD 1.92->3.20, AVXUSD 2.37->3.58 DESPITE adding cost - impossible on identical data), so those rankings are window noise. The three candidates below were the liquid instruments that stayed STABLE across both runs with tight measured spreads: USDCNH PF 1.797->1.787 (2.3p), GBPAUD 1.592->1.578 (0.9p), EURAUD ~1.42 (0.7p). AUDJPY and XAGUSD also repeated but are already enabled - confirmations, not candidates.
H022.method: H008c house standard, per symbol: download the deepest available H4 history (broker trendbars and/or Twelve Data, >=5y where it exists), run the FROZEN production strategy (no tuning) with the measured real spread as commission, on a chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%) split. Only the TEST slice counts. Also run the yearly-stability read (h4_yearly_stability style) on TEST years. Nothing in the live config changes while this runs.
H022.decision_rule: Written before any deep-history result exists. PER SYMBOL, independently: ADOPT-TO-DEMO only if (1) TEST-slice PF >= 1.2 with n >= 40 closed trades at real measured spread, AND (2) no TEST year's PF < 0.9. A symbol failing either -> REJECT, documented in the ledger. If deep history for a symbol is too short for n >= 40 on the TEST slice, verdict is INSUFFICIENT DATA - no enable, no forcing. ADOPT-TO-DEMO means: enabled: true in config/symbols.yaml with its own outcome bucket; its trades DO NOT count toward the pre-registered D001 (FX) or D002 (carriers) buckets, whose symbol sets stay frozen as registered - a new decision rule D003 covering these crosses may be registered at enable time, BEFORE any of their outcomes exist. Alt-coin candidates from the sweep are NOT covered by this hypothesis and stay out until a candidate shows >=3y of history AND cross-window rank stability.
H022.notes: Sweep PFs above are IN-SAMPLE screening signals only - they carry zero adoption weight by themselves (STRATEGY_EVIDENCE 07-06 sweep lesson). The whole point of this entry is that the deep OOS split, not the screen, makes the call.
H022.conclusion: Applied 2026-07-23 - the manifest existed since 2026-07-17 but the registry status was never updated to reflect it (found while auditing which PLANNED hypotheses are actually untested vs already resolved). Rule applied literally, per symbol: GBPAUD REJECT (TEST PF 0.825 < 1.2; TEST-year 2025 PF 0.337 << 0.9 - fails both conditions). EURAUD REJECT (TEST PF 0.887 < 1.2; TEST-year 2025 PF 0.424 << 0.9 - fails both conditions). USDCNH INSUFFICIENT_DATA (TEST n=18 < 40 floor; PF 1.926 looks promising but is not evaluable yet). Overall FAILED: the two symbols with enough data both reject clearly (not close misses), and both share a bad 2025 - a real regime-instability signal, not a sampling artifact, echoing the FX-12 book's general non-edge (p=0.078). USDCNH stays open only in the narrow sense that more history could someday clear the n>=40 floor; it is not a reason to revisit GBPAUD/EURAUD. None of the three symbols are ADOPT-TO-DEMO; config/symbols.yaml is unaffected (all three were never enabled).

H023.title: Wyckoff volume gating by asset class: after the cTrader migration gave FX bars nonzero tick-volume, does forcing FX back to price-only Wyckoff (vs the now-active tick-volume mode) change the system's out-of-sample edge?
H023.origin: Mechanism-motivated from code inspection (2026-07-18 engine review), NOT from a backtest result. wyckoff_engine._volume_analysis gates on `window['volume'].sum() == 0`: under the old Twelve Data Free feed FX volume was 0, so FX ran the engine's intended price-only Wyckoff. cTrader (now the primary fx/metals/energy/indices provider) returns nonzero tick-volume in the trendbar 'v' field (core/data_providers.py), so that gate no longer trips for FX and Wyckoff now runs full volume-spread analysis on FX TICK-volume - contradicting the engine's own docstring ('For FOREX: price-only Wyckoff ... only tick volume proxy'). The provider migration silently changed Wyckoff's FX behavior; this asks whether that change helps, hurts, or is noise. Wyckoff is one of prod4 (weight 0.0707).
H023.method: H008c house standard on the 7 enabled FX symbols (EURUSD, GBPUSD, USDJPY, USDCHF, EURJPY, GBPJPY, AUDJPY), chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%) split, FROZEN prod4 strategy with real measured spread as commission. Two arms on identical data, differing ONLY in Wyckoff's FX volume mode: arm A = current (tick-volume, gate as-is), arm B = asset-class gated (FX forced to price-only regardless of nonzero tick-volume). Primary metric = pooled TEST-slice profit factor of the FULL pipeline (not Wyckoff's internal sub-score) across the 7 FX symbols. Metals (XAUUSD, XAGUSD) and crypto (BTCUSD, ETHUSD) are unchanged controls - they legitimately carry real/tick volume - and are re-run under both arms only to confirm B does not disturb them. Nothing in the live config changes while this runs.
H023.decision_rule: Written before any result exists. ADOPT arm B (asset-class gate, FX->price-only) only if ALL hold on the TEST slice: (1) pooled FX PF(B) - PF(A) >= 0.10, (2) pooled FX TEST n >= 100 closed trades, (3) the sign of the per-symbol PF change is >= 0 in at least 5 of the 7 FX symbols (not one symbol carrying the pool), AND (4) controls not degraded: metals+crypto pooled TEST PF(B) >= PF(A) - 0.05. If any fails -> FAILED / NO CHANGE (the current tick-volume behavior stays, documented in the rejected ledger). A near-zero difference (|PF(B)-PF(A)| < 0.10) is itself a valid, committable NULL result - it would mean the silent behavior change is immaterial at system level, consistent with Wyckoff's small weight. REGARDLESS of the OOS verdict, this is measurement only: no live code change until the forward-demo evidence counter reaches its milestone (CLAUDE.md rule 6) - H023 is FROZEN like H018, so it can never reset the prospective counter mid-sample.
H023.notes: Wyckoff's weight is only 0.0707, so the system-level effect may well be negligible; the decision rule deliberately requires a SYSTEM-level (full-pipeline) improvement, not a Wyckoff-internal one, and treats a null as a real outcome. This entry is pre-registered from a code mechanism, exactly the legitimate origin the house standard allows - it is NOT a pick-the-winner from a subset/grid search (that path is on the dead list, H015).
H023.data_source_caveat: FIRST RUN (2026-07-23, VPS) INVALIDATED, NOT RECORDED AS A RESULT: the runner's original data discovery used data/{sym}_H1_{2y,5y}.csv (scripts/download_all_symbols.py, Yahoo Finance). Yahoo reports ZERO volume for every FX pair (confirmed on the VPS: load_from_csv(...)['volume'].describe() -> max 0.0) - so arm A already had zero FX volume, making arm B's 'zero the volume' intervention a no-op. Result was dPF=0.0 EXACTLY on all 7 symbols with identical trade counts per arm - the signature of a non-contrasting test, not a real measurement, so it was discarded rather than committed. Fix: execution/ctrader_client.py::get_trendbars() gained an additive to_timestamp_ms param (2026-07-24, backward-compatible, every other caller unaffected) enabling scripts/download_ctrader_fx_history.py to page back through cTrader's own trendbars, which DO carry real tick-volume for FX. The runner's discover() now requires data/{sym}_H1_ctrader.csv for FX symbols (falls back to the Yahoo file with a loud warning if absent) and aborts per-symbol if that file's volume is still >=50% zero (canary against the same failure mode recurring silently).
H023.conclusion: SECOND RUN (2026-07-24, VPS, real cTrader-sourced FX volume confirmed nonzero via --probe before the full run - mean=3844, min=71, max=14972 on EURUSD, not the zero from run 1) applied literally: pooled FX arm A PF=0.981 n=1089, arm B PF=0.981 n=1089, dPF=0.0 on ALL 7 symbols individually (EURUSD/GBPUSD/USDJPY/USDCHF/EURJPY/GBPJPY/AUDJPY), controls (metals+crypto, untouched by design) PF=1.35 n=326 both arms. Rule checks: (1) pooled dPF>=0.10 FAILS (0.0); (2) n>=100 PASSES (1089); (3) >=5/7 symbols sign>=0 PASSES (7/7, all exactly 0.0); (4) controls not degraded PASSES (identical). Since (1) fails, not ADOPT; |dPF|<0.10 with controls held -> NULL per the pre-registered rule. Mechanistically explicable, not a repeat of run 1's broken-test artifact: engines/wyckoff_engine.py:228-250 shows volume analysis only ADDS score on top of an ALREADY price-determined bias (spring/upthrust/range-position, lines 190-226) - it never flips the vote itself - and Wyckoff's confluence weight (0.0707) bounds its maximum possible swing on the weighted score to roughly 20*0.0707~=1.4 points, apparently never enough to cross min_score_to_trade or flip agree_count/winning_bias across 1089 real trades. Verdict: NULL - the cTrader-migration-induced silent behavior change (FX Wyckoff running full volume analysis instead of the intended price-only mode) is immaterial at system level, exactly as the pre-registered notes anticipated given Wyckoff's small weight. No live/config change (current tick-volume behavior stays as-is; there was never anything to revert since Wyckoff's live behavior is unchanged either way).

H024.title: Hard regime gate vs. current soft regime-weighting: the system reweights engines by regime (confluence/regime_weights.py) but never blocks on it. Does a hard gate emitting NO_TRADE when regime==RANGING (trade only TRENDING) beat the always-on soft-weighted baseline out-of-sample across the 20-symbol universe?
H024.origin: Operator-proposed (regime engine idea) + mechanism-motivated from code inspection. Finding: ~90% of the proposed 'regime engine' already exists and is LIVE — regimes/regime_detector.py (TRENDING/RANGING), regimes/volatility_classifier.py (ATR low/normal/high/extreme), regimes/session_context.py (London/NY), core/market_quality.py _market_quality_gate (already blocks on poor quality), and confluence/regime_weights.py (soft regime multipliers, hand-crafted + self-described as never validated). The genuinely un-measured lever is a HARD regime gate (block RANGING) vs the current SOFT weighting. Counter-prior stated up front: 2026-07-19 in-sample attribution (3238 trades) showed regime performance FLAT (RANGING WR 34.8% >= TRENDING 33.2%), so the naive 'trend-only is better' intuition is in-sample UNfavorable — which is exactly why a pre-registered OOS test is the arbiter.
H024.method: Two-arm A/B on identical bars, FROZEN prod4 pipeline, full 20-symbol universe (walk_forward_20260719 set), H4 decision TF + D1 MTF gate, real measured spread, chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%) (H008c standard) + walk-forward windows. Arm A = current (soft regime weighting, all regimes tradeable). Arm B = identical PLUS a hard gate that emits NO_TRADE when regime==RANGING. Entries/exits/stops/targets byte-identical between arms; only the take/skip decision differs. Runner: scripts/H024_regime_gate_ab.py (to be written), backtest_mode=True.
H024.decision_rule: ADOPT arm B (hard regime gate) ONLY if ALL hold on the pooled TEST slice: (1) pooled PF(B)-PF(A) >= 0.15; (2) arm B retains >= 50% of arm A TEST trade count (volume-collapse guard); (3) improvement in >= 60% of symbols individually (H015 cherry-pick guard); (4) carriers XAU/BTC/ETH pooled PF(B) >= PF(A)-0.05. Any failure => FAILED/NO CHANGE (soft weighting stays, logged in rejected ledger). |PF(B)-PF(A)| < 0.15 with volume retained = NULL (immaterial). Pre-registered minimum: pooled arm-A TEST n >= 300.
H024.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT H015 (engine-subset) — all prod4 engines stay enabled; H024 gates whether to trade at all, not which engines run. NOT H002/H002b (regime-filtered sweep ENTRY) — no entry pattern added; gates the unchanged confluence output. NOT trade-management A/B — exits untouched, byte-identical between arms.
H024.live_safety: Measurement only. Feature flag features.regime_gate default false (mirrors features.market_quality_gate). No live change until forward-demo milestone (rule 6). FROZEN like H018/H023 — cannot reset the prospective counter mid-sample.
H024.not_in_scope: Whether the existing hand-crafted regime_weights.py multipliers beat flat weights is a separate test (reserved H034: multipliers ON vs OFF, see research/hypotheses/BACKLOG_2026-07-21.md). H024 toggles exactly one thing — the hard gate — so its verdict is attributable.
H024.conclusion: NULL by the pre-registered rule, applied by the pre-built runner (2026-07-22 VPS run, scripts/H024_regime_gate_ab.py): pooled TEST PF(A)=1.12 (n=1187) vs PF(B)=1.096 (n=1000), dPF=-0.024 (needed >=+0.15), volume retention 0.842 (passed), B>A in only 42.1% of symbols (needed >=60%), carriers PF 1.335->1.256 (degradation 0.079 > the 0.05 allowed). Runner verdict verbatim: 'NULL (gate immaterial; soft weighting keeps edge)'. Reading: blocking RANGING removes ~16% of trades and the removed trades were carrying their weight - especially on the carriers, where the gate strictly hurt. Consistent with the 2026-07-19 in-sample attribution (RANGING WR >= TRENDING) that the registration flagged as the counter-prior. features.regime_gate stays false (it always was); soft regime weighting stays as-is; no live change. Result JSON on the VPS - freeze via scripts/revive_manifests.py from a clean tree, commit with the evidence set.

H025.title: Information compression: before large moves the H4 price path becomes more algorithmically ordered (lower LZ76 complexity of the sign-of-return sequence), not merely lower-volatility; gating the frozen prod4 pipeline to below-median-complexity states improves pooled OOS PF.
H025.origin: Operator-proposed 2026-07-21 (batch-1 'unconventional' ideas, operator id H025; also batch-2 c-H037/c-H058). Survives the dead-list check: price-only input (sign sequence of closes), no liquidity/volume/entry-pattern concepts. Complexity is a different axis from ATR/Bollinger width: a low-ATR window can be maximally random and vice versa. Honest counter stated up front: LZ complexity correlates with trend persistence and vol clustering; if the effect is just an ATR-squeeze proxy, the recorded middle-ATR-tercile diagnostic will show it.
H025.method: Two stages, strictly ordered. Frozen parameters (no forking paths): LZ76 on binary close-to-close sign sequence, window 64 H4 bars, normalized by n/log2(n); trailing 500-bar percentile per symbol; Stage-1 forward horizon 20 bars normalized by ATR(14); Stage-2 gate threshold percentile <= 0.50. Secondary measures (Shannon entropy 8-bin, zlib ratio) recorded only, never decision inputs. Stage 1 (TRAIN slice ONLY, 20-symbol universe, H008c chronological 65/35 split): does the bottom complexity quintile precede larger normalized moves? Stage 2 (only if Stage 1 passes): two-arm A/B on the FROZEN prod4 pipeline, identical bars, real measured spread; arm B adds NO_TRADE unless complexity percentile <= 0.50; entries/exits byte-identical. Runner: research/experiments/H025_information_compression.py (to be written).
H025.decision_rule: Stage 1 -> proceed only if ALL on TRAIN: (1) pooled bottom-quintile median forward-range/ATR >= 1.10x unconditional median; (2) block-bootstrap p < 0.05 (1000 resamples); (3) ratio > 1.0 in >= 60% of symbols. Stage-1 failure = FAILED, Stage 2 never runs, no OOS consumed. Stage 2 -> ADOPT arm B only if ALL on pooled TEST: (1) PF(B)-PF(A) >= 0.15; (2) arm B retains >= 50% of arm A TEST trades; (3) improvement in >= 60% of symbols; (4) carriers pooled PF(B) >= PF(A)-0.05. Any failure = FAILED/NO CHANGE. |dPF| < 0.15 with volume retained = NULL (real but immaterial). Minimums stated before running: Stage-1 pooled bottom-quintile n >= 500 bars; Stage-2 pooled arm-A TEST n >= 300 trades.
H025.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT H001/H002/H002b (no liquidity or equal-high/low concept; input is the close sign sequence only). NOT crypto_volume (price-only, no volume term). NOT the H008 entry family (arm B adds no entries, only withholds existing decisions). NOT operator idea 'energy accumulation' (b1-H028, rejected: its composite includes dead components; H025 isolates the single non-dead one).
H025.live_safety: Measurement only. Stage-2 gate behind features.complexity_gate default false (mirrors features.regime_gate). No live change regardless of verdict until the forward-demo milestone (rule 6). FROZEN like H018/H023/H024.
H025.not_in_scope: Which complexity measure is 'best' (measure selection = new hypothesis); intraday timeframes; using complexity as a sizing input rather than a gate.
H025.conclusion: FAILED at Stage 1 by the pre-registered rule (2026-07-21, VPS run, manifest h025_stage1_20260721): pooled bottom-quintile/unconditional forward-move ratio 1.0049 (needed >= 1.10) and symbol-cluster bootstrap p = 0.3090 (needed < 0.05). Only the breadth guard passed (9/15 symbols ratio > 1.0 = 60%). Effect size is zero to the third decimal: low LZ76 complexity carries no information about forward 20-bar range/ATR on this universe. The recorded mid-ATR-tercile diagnostic is ~1.0 as well, so nothing was hiding behind volatility conditioning - 'compression' here is not even a repackaged ATR squeeze; it is nothing. Coverage: 15/20 symbols evaluated (AUDUSD/USDCAD/NZDUSD/EURGBP/EURCHF absent from the deep-history download universe); pooled quintile n = 7353 >> the 500 minimum, so the verdict stands. Per the registered rule Stage 2 is never built: no complexity gate, no features.complexity_gate flag, no OOS data consumed. Secondary measures (entropy/zlib) recorded in the result JSON.

H033.title: Meta-confidence gate: a frozen-spec logistic meta-model trained only on decision-time features of the system's own historical simulated trades can rank future trades by win probability well enough that skipping the lowest-confidence 30% improves pooled OOS PF.
H033.origin: Operator-proposed 2026-07-21 (batch-1 operator id H033 'Meta-Engine'; batch-2 c-H045 'confidence calibration'). Meta-labeling applied to IATIS's own ledger - no market signal added; scores the frozen pipeline's decisions using only decision-time information. Prior honestly against it: every measured gate/addition so far cost edge or did nothing (7 gates shadow book, H015, H017), and the confluence score is already a hand-crafted confidence proxy with measured marginal value ~0. Operator's floated absolute 80% confidence cutoff replaced at registration by a percentile threshold: at ~34% base WR an absolute 0.80 cutoff would never trade.
H033.method: Frozen model spec, no tuning, no model search: L2 logistic regression C=1.0, sklearn defaults, one pooled model. Label = simulated trade outcome (target vs stop). Features (decision-time only): confluence score, per-engine agreement vector (prod4), regime, volatility class, session, asset class, ATR(14) percentile, D1 alignment, proposed RR. No symbol identity beyond asset class. Fit on TRAIN-slice trades of the frozen prod4 backtest (20-symbol universe, H008c 65/35 chronological split, real spread), FREEZE, then replay TEST decisions: arm B skips scores below the 30th percentile of TRAIN score distribution. Walk-forward refit as robustness check. Consumes the arm-A ledger produced by the H024/H025 harness - no new backtest engine needed. Runner: research/experiments/H033_meta_confidence_gate.py (to be written).
H033.decision_rule: Sanity gate first: frozen TRAIN-fit model must reach pooled TEST AUC >= 0.55, else FAILED regardless of PF (a PF win with AUC ~0.5 is luck). ADOPT arm B only if ALL on pooled TEST: (1) PF(B)-PF(A) >= 0.15; (2) arm B retains >= 50% of arm A TEST trades; (3) improvement in >= 60% of symbols; (4) carriers pooled PF(B) >= PF(A)-0.05. Any failure = FAILED/NO CHANGE. |dPF| < 0.15 with AUC >= 0.55 = NULL worth recording (system can rank itself but ranking not monetizable at this threshold). Minimums stated before running: pooled TRAIN n >= 1000 closed trades, pooled arm-A TEST n >= 300 (2026-07-19 run had 3238 trades, feasible). A second model family, hyperparameter, feature set, or threshold = a NEW hypothesis with a new ID.
H033.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT H015 (all prod4 engines stay enabled). NOT threshold/weight tuning (min_score, quorum, weights untouched in both arms; gate sits strictly after the frozen decision). NOT managed exits (exits byte-identical). NOT H017 (no new market-structure detector; inputs are the system's own outputs).
H033.live_safety: Measurement only. Behind features.meta_gate default false. Model fits on simulated history only, never trains on or alters the live demo book. No live change regardless of verdict until the forward-demo milestone (rule 6). FROZEN like H018/H023/H024.
H033.not_in_scope: Model selection/tuning; using the score for sizing; online/continual learning; feature-importance analysis (SHAP etc. is diagnostics tooling, not part of the verdict).
H033.conclusion: FAILED by the pre-registered sanity gate (2026-07-22 VPS run): TEST AUC 0.5071 (floor 0.55) - the frozen-spec logistic model cannot rank the system's own trades out of sample at all. Walk-forward recorded reads confirm it is noise around 0.5, not a threshold artifact: W1 AUC 0.548, W2 AUC 0.474 (the 'signal' flips sign between windows). PF deltas were immaterial anyway (arm A 1.12 n=1187 - identical to H024's arm A, a good harness-reproduction check - vs arm B 1.116, dPF -0.004) and the gate disproportionately skipped good carrier trades (carriers 1.335 -> 1.233). Institutional meaning: the decision-time features (confluence score, engine agreement, regime, session, ATR percentile, D1 alignment, RR) carry ~zero information about which EXECUTE decisions win. This is a coherence check on the frozen-thresholds stance - if the score could rank trades, threshold tuning would matter; it cannot, so it does not. Also weakens the priors of backlog H049 (analog memory), H043 (consensus dynamics) and adaptive-threshold ideas: they are recombinations of the same informationless features. features.meta_gate was never created; nothing live changes. The featured 3415-trade arm-A ledger (H033_trade_ledger.json) is preserved as a research asset.

H037.title: Decision delay: entering N in {1,2,3} H4 bars after an EXECUTE signal (same signals, same stop/target distances re-anchored to the delayed close) changes pooled OOS PF materially and robustly vs immediate entry.
H037.origin: Operator-proposed (2026-07-21 catalog c-H059; reserved H037 in research/hypotheses/BACKLOG_2026-07-21.md). Chosen as the top reserved item because it is cheap (one signal-capture pass, delay arms are pure geometry replays) and mechanically distinct from everything measured. Prior honestly against it: entry-timing tweaks are cousins of managed exits (OOS ~ 0) and H033 showed decision-time context cannot rank outcomes, so a uniform entry shift must work through price mechanics (systematic post-signal pullback) alone. Expected NULL; it runs because the mechanism is genuinely unmeasured.
H037.method: ONE pipeline pass captures arm A's decision set (i, entry, sl, tp, direction) on the H024/H033 harness (run_pipeline per bar, backtest_mode, H1 CSVs, ACTIVE_SYMBOLS, step 8, warmup 220, no-overlap). Delay arms B(N): entry = close of bar i+N, SL/TP re-anchored with the ORIGINAL signal-time distances, simulation from i+N+1, same no-overlap rule (signals landing inside prior occupancy drop). No invalidation filter - the wait is the only mechanism. Built-in validity check: a delay-0 replay must reproduce arm A exactly. Chronological TRAIN(65%)/TEST(35%); verdict on pooled TEST. Runner: research/experiments/H037_decision_delay.py.
H037.decision_rule: ADOPT the smallest passing N ONLY if ALL hold for that N on pooled TEST: (1) PF(B_N)-PF(A) >= 0.15; (2) B(N) retains >= 80% of arm A TEST trades (stricter than gate hypotheses - mechanism is unconditional); (3) improvement in >= 60% of symbols; (4) carriers pooled PF(B_N) >= PF(A)-0.05; (5) family-consistency: dPF > 0 for >= 2 of the 3 delays (one N passing while neighbors degrade = noise). Any failure = FAILED/NO CHANGE. All three |dPF| < 0.15 with retention held = NULL (entry timing immaterial at H4). Minimum: pooled arm-A TEST n >= 300.
H037.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT managed exits (exits byte-identical; only the entry anchor shifts). NOT the H008 entry family (no new pattern or condition; signal set is exactly arm A's). NOT a gate (nothing skipped by intent; guard 2 punishes mechanical drops). NOT threshold/weight tuning (all frozen).
H037.live_safety: Measurement only. No feature flag exists unless ADOPT, and an adopted delay still waits for the forward-demo milestone like H018 (rule 6) since it would reset the prospective counter.
H037.not_in_scope: Conditional delays (wait only if X), intrabar fills, delays > 3 bars, delay as a function of regime/volatility - each would be a NEW hypothesis.
H037.conclusion: NULL by the pre-registered rule, applied literally (2026-07-24 VPS run, scripts/H037_decision_delay's built-in validity check passed on every symbol - delay-0 replay reproduced arm A's captured trades exactly). Arm A pooled TEST PF=1.12 n=1187 - identical to H024's and H033's arm-A baseline on the same shared harness, a third independent confirmation of harness fidelity, not a coincidence to be suspicious of. Per-delay TEST results: B(1) PF=1.083 n=1060 dPF=-0.037 retention=0.893 symbol_win_frac=0.579 carriers=1.230; B(2) PF=1.041 n=1029 dPF=-0.079 retention=0.867 symbol_win_frac=0.263 carriers=1.188; B(3) PF=1.027 n=1006 dPF=-0.093 retention=0.848 symbol_win_frac=0.316 carriers=1.085. No delay reaches the ADOPT bar (dPF>=+0.15) - all three are negative. All three |dPF|<0.15 with retention>=80% held on every delay -> NULL: entry timing is immaterial at H4. Direction is monotonically negative and worsening with longer delays, and symbol_win_frac collapses well below the 60% cherry-pick guard at every delay - waiting does not 'buy a better entry', if anything the drift is mildly adverse, just not large enough to cross into FAILED territory beyond NULL. Matches the hypothesis's own pre-stated expectation ('expected NULL or worse') - an honestly-arrived-at null, not a reframed surprise. No feature flag was ever created (none is created unless ADOPT). Measurement only; nothing live changed (CLAUDE.md rule 6).

H103.title: Does confluence/meta_decision.py's live confidence-gate (BLOCK when confidence<40, downgrades EXECUTE to NO_TRADE) improve OOS PF, or does it just double-count already-gated information and cost good trades for free?
H103.origin: docs/PHILOSOPHY_AUDIT_2026-07.md:83,144,191,240 flags meta_decision.py as structural double-counting - it recombines the confluence score (already gated by min_score_to_trade) and agree_count (already gated by min_engines_agreeing) into a 'confidence' that gates a third time - and recommends removal. Operator plan review (this session, 2026-07-23) proposed the same removal; PLANNED here instead of executed directly because unlike H013 reversal_veto (a proven no-op removed the same day), meta_decision is NOT dead: main.py:497-500 shows it actively flips EXECUTE->NO_TRADE today using live prod4 engines. Strong prior against the gate having real value: H033 (FAILED, 2026-07-22) trained a logistic model on this EXACT feature family - confluence score, engine agreement, regime/session/ATR context - and it could not rank the system's own trades OOS at all (TEST AUC 0.5071, floor 0.55). meta_decision._confidence_score() is a hand-crafted, untrained linear-ish combination of a subset of those same features (30% score + 30% stability + 20% agreement + 20% data quality) - if a fitted model on richer inputs has ~zero OOS ranking power, an unfitted formula on fewer inputs is not expected to do better. But H033 tested a different mechanism (a trained meta-model) - it does not itself falsify meta_decision.py, hence a dedicated test rather than inferring removal directly from H033.
H103.method: Controlled A/B on the frozen prod4 pipeline (H008c 65/35 chronological TRAIN/TEST split, real spread, 20-symbol universe - same harness pattern as H024/H033 arm-A). Arm A = current live behavior (meta_decision computed and allowed to downgrade EXECUTE->NO_TRADE on BLOCK verdict). Arm B = meta_decision still computed and logged for comparison, but its BLOCK verdict never downgrades the decision (entries/exits/thresholds/all other gates byte-identical between arms - isolates only this one gate, same discipline as H017/H024). Minimum pooled arm-A TEST n >= 300 before the rule below is evaluated.
H103.decision_rule: Written before any run. REMOVE the gate (adopt arm B / delete the downgrade in main.py) only if, on pooled TEST: (1) PF(B) >= PF(A) - 0.03 (removing it does not cost material edge - if it were doing real work, B should be visibly worse); AND (2) carriers-only PF(B) >= PF(A) - 0.03 (the one book that matters per CLAUDE.md must not degrade); AND (3) trade count(B) > trade count(A) (confirms the gate was actually blocking something, not vacuous). KEEP the gate (FAILED - do not remove) if PF(B) drops by more than 0.03 pooled or on carriers specifically - that would mean the double-counting story is wrong and the gate is doing real, measurable filtering. |dPF| < 0.03 with no material n difference = NULL, keep as-is (not worth the governance churn of removing something inert-but-harmless). A different feature set, formula, or trained-model version of this gate is a NEW hypothesis with a new ID, not a rerun of H103.
H103.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT H033 (that tested a NEW trained logistic model as a gate; this tests whether the EXISTING hand-crafted, already-live meta_decision.py gate should be removed - different mechanism, though H033's finding is the motivating prior). NOT H013/reversal_veto (that gate was a proven structural no-op removed outright the same day - this one is live and must be measured, not assumed).
H103.live_safety: Measurement only, on the backtest harness - zero live/config change until this hypothesis resolves and is promoted through the normal process. main.py's live meta_decision gate stays exactly as-is regardless of how long this takes. Same FROZEN discipline as H018/H023/H024/H033: do not let a plan review's plausible-sounding critique become a live change without its own OOS evidence.
H103.implementation: Built 2026-07-24: main.py's meta_decision downgrade now gated behind features.meta_decision_gate (config.yaml, default TRUE — preserves today's live behavior exactly, unlike the default-FALSE pattern used for genuinely new mechanisms like H019/H024, since this gate is already live and removal is the one-sided change that needs evidence). meta is ALWAYS computed regardless of the flag (so arm B can still log/compare it); the flag only controls whether a BLOCK verdict is allowed to downgrade EXECUTE->NO_TRADE. 4 wiring tests (tests/test_axis8_and_downgrade.py) pin: default-absent-key behaves as gate=True, explicit gate=False lets BLOCK through while still computing meta, explicit gate=True matches the default, and a PASS verdict is unaffected by the flag either way. A/B harness: research/experiments/H103_meta_decision_gate_ab.py, following the H024/H037 run_pipeline()-based pattern (two full pipeline passes, flag flipped between arms, chronological 65/35 split) rather than H033's trained-model harness, since this mechanism is a live boolean toggle, not a model to fit. 8 unit tests (tests/test_h103_meta_decision_gate_ab.py) pin the SYMMETRIC decision rule (ADOPT/FAILED-degraded/NULL/FAILED-default/INSUFFICIENT_DATA branches) against synthetic trade pools. Not yet run — needs the same H1 CSVs as H024/H033/H037, VPS-only.

H104.title: Crypto tick-level order-flow imbalance (cumulative delta divergence) as an independent entry signal for BTCUSD/ETHUSD, distinct from the rejected bar-level volume input
H104.origin: Operator plan review (2026-07-23). Explicitly NOT a re-run of the crypto_volume experiment (research/results/crypto_volume_20260706_manifest.json, dead list: ccxt raw traded volume fed into existing engines as a feature, DeltaPF -0.016 BTC / 0.000 ETH - that test has no H-number in this registry, contrary to a plan citation that called it 'H019'; H019 is a separate, still-PLANNED hypothesis about funding-rate/OI/Fear&Greed positioning, thematically adjacent but a different mechanism from both this and the dead volume test). H104 differs from the dead test on three axes: (1) tick/aggTrade granularity vs bar-level volume; (2) aggressor-side classification (buy-initiated vs sell-initiated) vs an undifferentiated volume total; (3) an independent entry signal with its own logic vs a feature bolted onto an existing engine's score.
H104.hypothesis_statement: Cumulative Delta (CD) divergence on a 15min aggregation (price makes a new H4 high while CD does not confirm, or vice versa) predicts mean-reversion within the next 4-8 H4 candles on BTCUSD/ETHUSD, with PF >= 1.3 after realistic costs (taker fee 0.04% + spread).
H104.data_source: Binance WebSocket aggTrade stream (wss://stream.binance.com:9443/ws/<symbol>@aggTrade) -> local tick store -> 15min CD aggregation. New data pipeline, not yet built.
H104.decision_rule: Written before any data collection. PASS (promote to RESEARCH, eligible for demo paper-trading): pooled OOS n >= 300 (research/edge_gate.py PROMOTION_CRITERIA, not the operator draft's n>=100 - kept consistent with every other engine's bar) AND PF >= 1.3 AND bootstrap p < 0.05. FAIL: PF < 1.1, or n < 150 after 3 months of live tick collection (data-availability floor distinct from the OOS-trade floor). INCONCLUSIVE (extend 60 days, re-evaluate, does not reset the clock): between FAIL and PASS thresholds.
H104.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT crypto_volume A/B (bar-level aggregate volume as an existing-engine feature, dead). NOT H019 (funding rate/open interest/Fear&Greed positioning proxies - aggregated sentiment, not tick-level order flow). A genuinely untested mechanism per the audit trail in this registry.
H104.live_safety: Research/data-collection only. No engine exists yet, no config flag, no live-path change. Building the tick collector and 15min CD aggregation is infrastructure work that can start before the decision rule is evaluated (the rule only gates PROMOTION, not data collection) - but no signal from this may influence any live or demo decision until PASS.
H104.data_collection: scripts/collect_binance_orderflow.py + iatis-orderflow-collector.service (2026-07-24) — the tick collector this hypothesis's own decision rule requires before any n exists (Binance's REST API only serves recent trades, not a historical backfill, unlike H019/H022's deep-history downloads; a backtestable sample only exists after this service has been running continuously). Long-running WebSocket service (Type=simple, Restart=always), not a timer — holds one persistent aggTrade connection per symbol (BTCUSD/ETHUSD), appends one 15min cumulative-delta bar per symbol to data/{SYMBOL}_orderflow_15min.jsonl (gitignored) on each completed bar. Pure logic (DeltaAccumulator, bar_start_ms, parse_agg_trade) unit-tested in tests/test_collect_binance_orderflow.py; the async connection loop itself is untested (needs a live network). Not yet enabled on the VPS as of this note — enabling it starts the ~3-month data-collection clock this hypothesis's decision_rule specifies; no verdict is possible before then.

H105.title: Does closing the gap between the strategy's reference entry price (decision-bar close) and the actual broker fill (next scheduler tick, live market order) cost measurable edge, and can a limit order reduce it?
H105.origin: Operator plan review (2026-07-23) proposed 'limit entry at H4_open - 0.3xATR instead of market-open entry'. Corrected before registration: verified in main.py:369 that the strategy's reference entry price is the CLOSE of the last decision-timeframe bar (df_base['close'].iloc[-1]), not the bar's open - and the real cTrader fill happens later, at whatever live spot price exists when the scheduler (interval=120min per iatis-scheduler.service, not synced to H4 boundaries) next runs execute_from_report(). The actual untested gap is reference-price-vs-live-fill-lag, not open-vs-limit within a single candle as originally framed.
H105.hypothesis_statement: Placing a limit order at the decision-bar close price (the strategy's own reference price) instead of an immediate market order at scheduler-tick time reduces the average entry cost (vs the reference price) by >= 0.15 ATR, without missing more than 15% of trades that would otherwise have filled (price moving away before the limit fills).
H105.method: Paper/shadow measurement first (no execution change): log, for every EXECUTE decision, the reference close price, the scheduler-tick timestamp, and the actual cTrader fill price already captured via TCA (storage/execution_quality.py, live since 2026-07-16) - compute the realized slippage distribution directly from data already being collected, before building any limit-order logic.
H105.decision_rule: Written before analysis. PASS (build limit-order execution path): TCA data shows mean adverse slippage >= 0.15 ATR-equivalent across >= 100 real fills. FAIL (not worth building): mean adverse slippage < 0.05 ATR-equivalent, or the distribution is not consistently adverse (i.e., current market-order timing is not systematically costly). Between: INCONCLUSIVE, keep accumulating TCA data.
H105.distinct_from_prior_kill: NOT managed exits / partial TP / trailing stops (dead list: trade-management A/B, +100% was a look-ahead artifact, OOS ~= 0) - H105 only concerns entry fill quality, exits are untouched. NOT a correlation cap (BTCUSD/ETHUSD already grouped in risk/correlation_engine.py RISK_ASSETS and gated via correlated_exposure_pct - already live, not a new idea).
H105.live_safety: Stage 1 (measurement) uses TCA data already being collected live - no code or config change. Stage 2 (building limit-order logic) only starts if Stage 1's decision rule PASSes, and even then stays behind a new config flag, demo-only, until its own forward evidence accumulates - same discipline as every other live-path change (CLAUDE.md rule 6).

walk_forward_validation.conclusion: Edge is real and generalizes to unseen data. 18/18 test windows above PF=1.5. This is statistically significant (p<0.0001). IATIS has a provable edge on FX majors, crypto, and energy.
warnings: USOIL W3 WR=44.8% in 2026 — possible weakening
Data limited to 2yr H1 — longer history would strengthen conclusion
No slippage modeled — real results will be lower
